heading: Abandoned and inactive noncoal mine restoration
label: 2336 Abandoned and inactive noncoal mine restoration

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical, planning, and design assistance to Federal and non-Federal interests for carrying out projects to address water quality problems caused by drainage and related activities from abandoned and inactive noncoal mines.
managing drainage from abandoned and inactive noncoal mines;
restoring and protecting streams, rivers, wetlands, other waterbodies, and riparian areas degraded by drainage from abandoned and inactive noncoal mines; and

demonstrating management practices and innovative and alternative treatment technologies to minimize or eliminate adverse environmental effects associated with drainage from abandoned and inactive noncoal mines.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: c Non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of assistance under subsection (a) shall be 50 percent, except that the Federal share with respect to projects located on land owned by the United States, on land held in trust by the Secretary of the Interior on behalf of, and for the benefit of, an Indian Tribe, or on restricted land of any Indian Tribe, shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: Effect on authority of Secretary of the Interior
children.label: d Effect on authority of Secretary of the Interior
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the authority of the Secretary of the Interior under title IV of the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 ( et seq.).30 U.S.C. 1231

children.heading: Technology database for reclamation of abandoned mines
children.label: e Technology database for reclamation of abandoned mines
children.text: The Secretary may provide assistance to non-Federal and nonprofit entities to develop, manage, and maintain a database of conventional and innovative, cost-effective technologies for reclamation of abandoned and inactive noncoal mine sites. Such assistance shall be provided through the Restoration of Abandoned Mine Sites Program managed by the Albuquerque District Office of the Corps of Engineers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $50,000,000.
